There is a LOT of things in kubejs that NEED to be in the right folders. You could have all your code correct and nothing will work. Things like recipe scripts actual game function changes functions on player join command creation etc Lets start off by learing where you can find your mod files and kubejs files The biggest thing you need to know is how to find your game files. For me im going to show this in CurseForge but it may differ from launcher to launcher Lets start by actually going into your modpack in CurseForge and right next to the play button you will see 3 little dots Now click on open folder

Now that we are in your modpack directory you need to find a folder called Kubejs, that is where ALL of your work will be done Inside the Kubejs folder you will see a few different folders and a ReadMe file, the few files you will more than likely be worried about will be the server_scripts and startup_scripts. This will be where most of your code will be ran, made, or messed with Both the Server_Script and the startup_scripts folder will usually have a file in them by default. In different version they may be names something different but in this example they are called main.js in the startup_scripts and main.js in the server_scripts but are sometimes names example.js

To start making your own file or script, it is very simple. You can start out by using the example one that is created for you at first launch but when you are ready to create a NEW file it is very simple. Start out by going into one of the two folders that we spoke about before (startup_scripts/Server_scripts) Start out by right clicking anywhere in the folder and go down to NEW then click on new text document Once you get a new file, rename it to something relative to what you are changing and change the .txt to .js When clicking ENTER it will ask you if you are sure you would like to change the file type from .txt to .js, just click yes

When coding in kubejs or any coding related things for minecraft you are going to need to know the namespaces of items, blocks, machines, fluids, etc. To easier find them when you are in game Press F3 + H to enable advanced tool tips in game and Now when you hover over any block, item, fluid, etc you will see a new name show up looking like minecraft:oak_log instead of just oak_log these are the names you will be using in code to tell the game what items you are wanting to use. Examples are here of before and after the advanced tool tips are enabled

In a main code/script file we will start with basics of coding. The main line of code that all scripts need when creating a new recipe or changing a recipe is a ServerEvents.recipes(event => { This line tells KubeJS: “Run this code when Minecraft is loading or updating recipes.”:
ServerEvents → This is a built-in KubeJS system. It means “We are listening for server-side game events.”
recipes blocks items loot tables
documents.s: The server is the part of Minecraft that controls gameplay logic like:

documents.t: .recipes → This tells KubeJS exactly what event we care about “We only want to modify crafting recipes.” So this line means “When Minecraft is handling recipes…”

This is EXAMPLE 1 (example is in neoforge 1.21.1) Create a file at :\Instances\Mod pack\kubejs\server_scripts: grass.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.shapeless('minecraft:grass_block', [ 'minecraft:dirt', 'minecraft:dirt' ]) })
documents.s: Create your first ITEM recipe (in server_scripts) NeoForge 1.21.1

documents.t: This is EXAMPLE 2 (example is in forge 1.20.1) Create a file at :\Instances\Mod pack\kubejs\server_scripts: grass.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.shapeless('minecraft:grass_block', [ 'minecraft:dirt', 'minecraft:dirt' ]) })
documents.s: Create your first ITEM recipe (in server_scripts) FORGE 1.20.1

documents.t: This is EXAMPLE 3 (example is in fabric 1.20.1) WARNING Server_Script dont show up until after the first world is loaded in this version of fabric Create a file at :\Instances\Mod pack\kubejs\server_scripts: grass.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.shapeless('minecraft:grass_block', [ 'minecraft:dirt', 'minecraft:dirt' ]) })
documents.s: Create your first ITEM recipe (in server_scripts) Fabric 1.20.1

documents.t: ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.shaped('minecraft:grass_block', [ 'DD ', ' ', ' ' ], { D: 'minecraft:dirt' }) }) To make a shaped recipe it is pretty much just like the shapless just with a few extra steps. The biggest thing is event.shaped('minecraft:grass_block', [ This tells minecraft that it is a shaped recipe, and sense there is event.shaped instead event.custom it automatically uses the crafting table. the code part that looks like 'DD ', ' ', ' ' is just telling the game what shape to put the items in the crafting table. Look at spaces inbeteen the '' and think of thoes as just empty spots in the crafting table This is what we call "Keys" This just gives a name of something to a letter. What ever item you give to this letter is what will be used in the crafting table!!! { D: 'minecraft:dirt' })

There is many different ways you can craft things using chaped crafting, here are three more examples of some fun things to doc // Vertical Recipe Example ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.shaped('minecraft:diamond', [ 'I ', 'I ', 'I ' ], { I: 'minecraft:iron_ingot' }) }) // Corner Recipe Example ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.shaped('minecraft:gold_ingot', [ 'G ', ' G ', ' G' ], { G: 'minecraft:gold_nugget' }) }) // Full Square Recipe Example ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.shaped('minecraft:emerald', [ 'EEE', 'EEE', 'EEE' ], { E: 'minecraft:emerald_block' }) })

Removing unwanted recipes is very common in modpacks. All removal scripts go in the kubejs/server_scripts/ folder. // File: server_scripts/remove_recipes.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.remove// Your removal code goes here }); This is specifically what you need to remove recipes in bulk, sings, by tags, and so on. Check the lower examples to see more information event.remove

Example 1, Removing the output of a item being crafted // File: server_scripts/remove_recipes.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { // Remove all ways to craft a Diamond Pickaxe event.remove({ output: 'minecraft:diamond_pickaxe' }) }) Example 2, same as example 1 just with a different item // File: server_scripts/remove_recipes.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { // Remove all ways to craft a Diamond Pickaxe event.remove({ output: 'minecraft:iron_ingot' }) })

Example 3, This is how you can remove the outputs or recipes of EVERY item, block, or tool that is in a specific mod. This example shows you it working with the Create mod. // File: server_scripts/remove_recipes.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { // Remove all ways to craft a Diamond Pickaxe event.remove({ output: 'minecraft:iron_ingot' }) })
documents.s: Removing ALL recipes in ANY mod

documents.t: Example 4, This is how you remove a recipe if it uses a specific item or material as its input or ingredient, This example shows me removing any recipe that uses oak planks as an ingredient, so every block or item in the game that uses oak planks no longer have a crafting recipe // File: server_scripts/remove_recipes.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { // Remove any recipe that uses Oak Planks as an ingredient event.remove({ input: 'minecraft:oak_planks' }); })

Example 5, This specific code shows you how to remove the output recipes of a entire block. in this case it shows you that if you do this with the campfire then you will no longer be allowed to put anything food wise on the camp fire and cook it. It completely removes cooking on the campfire // File: server_scripts/remove_recipes.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { // Remove all campfire cooking recipes event.remove({ type: 'minecraft:campfire_cooking' }); })
documents.s: Removing ALL types of output for a specific block (the campfire outputs)

documents.t: Example 5, this example shows that you can remove specific outputs also and not just ALL outputs. this shows that you can disable or remove smelting a ore or raw ore like raw iron in a furnace but still have it working in ONLY the blast furnace AND this also works for the camp fire // File: server_scripts/remove_recipes.js // Remove all blasting recipes for iron ServerEvents.recipes(event => { // Remove all campfire cooking recipes event.remove({ type: 'minecraft:smelting', output: 'minecraft:iron_ingot' }) }) ,
documents.s: Removing SPECIF types of output for a specific block (the fuirnace outputs and blast furnace outputs)

documents.t: There is a few things in the default Create mod you can modify with kubjs and its all very starter friendly. Lets look at some examples of things EVERYONE changes

mechanical crafters Millstone Deployers Including sequenced assembly deploying stripping automative crafting Mechanical Saw wood cutting crafting stone cutting glass cutting crafting Item Drain Draining items into fluids and items Mechanical Mixer automated brewing automatic shapless crafting Muxing (crafting) Including heated and non heated recipes Mechanical Press Automated Packing (ITEMS - shaped crafting) Compacting (auto crafting W Items/Fluids) Pressing (things like) -iron sheets dirt into path blocks etc. Encased Fans Including haunting (soul campfire and soul fire) smoking (camp fire and fire) washing blasting and sand paper

This may look like a LOT of reading, but i promis this will be worth it. We will show you an example of everything here. There should also be different versions of each example of the code to work on multiple different versions sense loads of things change between FORGE, NEOFORGE, and fabric. So be sure to reach wha version this code is for before trying it.

forge 1.20.1 In this example we show you how to make a very unfair recipe, but fun none the less. You need to fill up a 4X4 set of mechanical crafters to make 1 single oak plank. Very annoying but this is a great recipe mechanical_crafters.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ type: "create:mechanical_crafting", pattern: [ "AAAA", "AAAA", "AAAA", "AAAA" ], key: { A: { item: "minecraft:oak_log" } }, result: { item: "minecraft:oak_planks", count: 1 } }) })

forge 1.20.1 this recipe shows you how to have a input to the millestone turn into something as the output. this one specifically shows you putting 1 log into the millestone and getting a plank out as the output Millstone.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:milling", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks", "chance": 1 } ], "processingTime": 1 }) })

forge 1.20.1 this recipe shows you how to have 1 item as the input (deployer holding) and another item on the belt and how to get a output from all of that. This shows you how to take a Log being held, deploying it onto another Log and getting a plank as the output deploying.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:deploying", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" }, { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks", "count": 1 } ] }) })

forge 1.20.1 This recipe shows you how to take a input such as oak_logs and making a output of stone. The output and input can be most items and blocks you want Mechanical_saw.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:cutting", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:stone", "count": 1 } ], "processingTime": 50 }) })

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to take a item IN to the drain and output two things, You absolutly have to have a fluid output. if you want 2 items the second one has to be a item, not another fluid. The drain can only hold up to one fluid at a time. Just make sure you 100% of the time have some sort of fluid coming out as a result or you will crash your game and the recipe wont work drain.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ type: "create:emptying", ingredients: [ { item: "minecraft:wheat" } ], results: [ { item: "minecraft:diamond" }, { fluid: "minecraft:water", amount: 1000 } ] }) })

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to take a item INTO a basin and mix it with the mechanical mixer non heated for a specific output, this one shows you how to put in oak_logs and get oak_planks as a result mixing.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:mixing", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" }, { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks", "count": 1 } ], "processingTime": 100 }) })

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to take a item INTO a basin and mix it with the mechanical mixer heated for a specific output, this one shows you how to put in oak_planks and get netherrack as a result heated_mixing.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:mixing", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks" }, { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:netherrack", "count": 1 } ], "processingTime": 100, "heatRequirement": "heated" }) })

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to take a item INTO a basin and mix it with the mechanical mixer SUPER heated for a specific output, this one shows you how to put in oak_planks and get netherrack as a result super_heated_mixing.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:mixing", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks" }, { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:netherrack", "count": 1 } ], "processingTime": 100, "heatRequirement": "superheated" }) })

*now its time to learn the one things that people LOVE and also get confused on. Encased Fans can be amazing to use for many different reasons BUT the name spacing for different types of things can confuse people. So here are a few recipe types you can do with these haunting create:haunting washing create:splashing Smoking (Fan + Fire) create:smoking Blasting (Fan + Lava) create:blasting

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to do haunting and this is by putting a soul fire or soul camp fire infront of the encased Fans and getting a output of some sort. this example shows Oak_logs going in and oak_planks coming out haunting.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:haunting", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks", "count": 1 } ] }) })

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to do smoking/cooking and this is by putting a fire or camp fire infront of the encased Fans and getting a output of some sort. this example shows Oak_logs going in and oak_planks coming out smoking.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.smoking( 'minecraft:oak_planks', 'minecraft:oak_log' ) })

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to do Washing and this is by putting water infront of the encased Fans and getting a output of some sort. this example shows Oak_logs going in and oak_planks coming out washing.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:splashing", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks", "count": 1 } ] }) })

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to do blasting and this is by putting lava infront of the encased Fans and getting a output of some sort. this example shows smooth_stone going in and oak_planks coming out bulk_blasting.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.blasting( 'minecraft:oak_planks', 'minecraft:smooth_stone' ) })

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to do sanding and this is rubbing something against sandpaper to get a output In this example you see oak_logs being sanded into oak_planks bulk_blasting.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:sandpaper_polishing", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks" } ] }) })

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to do crushing and this when you have two crushing wheels spinning inwards very quick and put two items in and get some items out. This is typically used for automatic ore creation or duplication bulk_blasting.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:crushing", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks", "count": 4 }, { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks", "count": 2, "chance": 0.5 } ], "processingTime": 150 }) })

In a main code/script file we will start with basics of coding. The main line of code that all scripts need when creating a new recipe or changing a recipe is a ServerEvents.recipes(event => { This line tells KubeJS: “Run this code when Minecraft is loading or updating recipes.”:
ServerEvents → This is a built-in KubeJS system. It means “We are listening for server-side game events.”
recipes blocks items loot tables
documents.s: The server is the part of Minecraft that controls gameplay logic like:

documents.t: .recipes → This tells KubeJS exactly what event we care about “We only want to modify crafting recipes.” we are using create fabric for the fabric version, and we are using create (the normal versions) for the rest. Just to teach how to use fluids, and what is different between versions

This is EXAMPLE 1 (example is in neoforge 1.21.1) Create a file at :\Instances\Mod pack\kubejs\server_scripts: grass.js in all honestly fluids are beyond broken in neoforge (no code to give at this moment)
documents.s: Create your first FLUID recipe (in server_scripts) NeoForge 1.21.1

documents.t: This is EXAMPLE 2 (example is in forge 1.20.1) Create a file at :\Instances\Mod pack\kubejs\server_scripts: water_bucket.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:mixing", "ingredients": [ { "fluid": "create:honey", amount: 500 }, { "fluid": "create:chocolate", amount: 500 }, ], "results": [ { "fluid": "minecraft:water", amount: 1000 } ] })})
documents.s: Create your first FLUID recipe (in server_scripts) FORGE 1.20.1

documents.t: This is EXAMPLE 3 (example is in fabric 1.20.1) WARNING Server_Script dont show up until after the first world is loaded in this version of fabric Create a file at :\Instances\Mod pack\kubejs\server_scripts: water_bucket.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:mixing", "ingredients": [ { "fluid": "create:honey", amount: 500 }, { "fluid": "create:chocolate", amount: 500 }, ], "results": [ { "fluid": "minecraft:water", amount: 1000 } ] })})
documents.s: Create your first FLUID recipe (in server_scripts) Fabric 1.20.1

documents.t: YOU DO NOT NEED THESE BUT THEY ARE NICE TO HAVE loot.js version may depend on what version you are playing on: kube.js Create version may depend on what version you are playing on: kube.js Mekanism version may depend on what version you are playing on: kube.js Additions version may depend on what version you are playing on: kube.js Powah version may depend on what version you are playing on: kube.js Delight version may depend on what version you are playing on: kube.js Ars Nouveau version may depend on what version you are playing on:
